signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Shock
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5736 — USD dominates as the strongest G10 performer (+1.188%) following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Warsh’s hawkish Fed debut. NZD is the G10 laggard (-0.894%) despite a hawkish RBNZ bias (OIS hike priced for 8 Jul). The 2.082% G10 spread reflects a flight to USD liquidity amid maritime corridor disruption. USD/NZD 150bp carry advantage and USD CESI (+30 net beats) reinforce the divergence. Watch for RBNZ delivery to trigger a short-squeeze on COT LF net short -22,354.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Volatility Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.23 — Price holds above the 160.00 threshold that previously triggered BoJ warnings; intervention risk is acute. 265bp SOFR-TONA carry sustains the bid, but options market is pricing heavy protection: IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.5% (VRP +4.7%) with a -1.16 moderate put skew (25d RR). Retail is 80% short, diverging from COT LF net short -115,036. Carry dominates until VIX > 25, but the 160+ level invites immediate MoF action.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Positioning + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4163 — Pair exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (corr -0.508 vs norm -0.005, z=-1.94σ), with USD strength decoupling from energy prices. Retail is 95% short (extreme), fading the institutional move while COT LF net short -60,671 remains crowded. 133bp SOFR-CORRA carry favors USD. Sustained break above 1.4200 targets retail capitulation. Watch Canadian CPI on Tuesday for CAD repricing.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Real Rate Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7014 — Extreme volatility premium (IV 15.3% vs HV30 7.8%, VRP +7.5%) signals market pricing for a sharp move despite AUD's 72bp OIS carry advantage. Real rates show AUD (+0.17%) is barely restrictive compared to USD (+1.48%), limiting AUD upside. AUD CESI (-8 net misses) confirms data underperformance. 25d RR at -0.92 confirms put skew; market is paying for downside protection despite COT LF net long +38,092.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Carry Neutrality + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3193 — Carry is neutral (SOFR 3.63% vs SONIA 3.73%, -10bp), leaving the pair sensitive to macro shifts. Options market shows caution with a -0.82 put skew (25d RR) and elevated VRP (+4.4%). GBP 1W avg performance (-0.855%) reflects broad USD strength rather than idiosyncratic GBP weakness. Watch for a break of 1.3150 to confirm downside momentum toward the next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced).
